commit.message:
Add build-time prerendering and fix title tag lengths for SEO

- Add prerender.js that generates per-route static HTML at build time
  with unique title, description, canonical, OG/Twitter tags, and
  keyword-rich body content for each of the 8 crawlable routes
- Each prerendered page has a single H1, proper nav, and footer
- Build command now runs `vite build && node prerender.js`
- Vercel filesystem-first routing serves prerendered pages to crawlers
- Shorten all page titles to under 60 characters to prevent Google
  truncation (was 73 chars on homepage)
- Zero runtime dependencies added (pure Node.js string templating)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix all paths to use BASE_URL for GitHub Pages deployment

Prefix all internal links, images, and asset references with
import.meta.env.BASE_URL so the site works under /london_grove_commons/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Perfect nav consistency + fix Gemini timeouts + more workers

Nav header (all 9 pages):
- Standardize height to 60px everywhere (dashboard/contact/terms/privacy were 56px)
- Remove mobile nav-brand font-size shrink in index.html (brand stays 1.1rem)
- Standardize Sign In button: padding 8px 18px, font-size 0.8rem, transition 0.2s
  with lift-on-hover across landing + calculator pages
- Add border-bottom to calculator nav (matches landing)
- Expand nav horizontal padding to clamp(16px,4vw,40px) on all pages
- Restore cta-note to "USITC 2026 Rev 4 data · No login required"
  (previously removed entire line; only 'Free forever...' portion wanted)

Crash fix:
- Add request_options={"timeout": 20} to both Gemini generate_content calls
  so a hung API call fails fast instead of blocking a worker for 60s+
- Bump gunicorn workers 2 → 4 so one slow request can't starve all traffic
- Reduce gunicorn timeout 120s → 60s (Gemini now times out at 20s)

Analyze, validate, QC, monitor streams; fingerprint audio; autotag;
compare streams; encode presets. Pure Rust JSON-RPC stdio server.

commit.message:
Cache Selenium session cookie to avoid re-login on every run

Save the session cookie and its expiry to .session_cache.json after
login. On subsequent runs, reuse the cached cookie until it expires,
then fall back to Selenium login automatically.

commit.message:
fix: move vercel.json to apps/web for correct monorepo deployment

Root vercel.json caused Vercel to read the root package.json (no Next.js),
failing framework detection. Moving config to apps/web means:
- Vercel Root Directory = apps/web → detects Next.js correctly
- Build command goes up to repo root to run full turbo pipeline
- Install command goes up to root to install all workspace deps
- Output directory defaults to .next (Next.js default, relative to apps/web)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: ; split now vertical (left|right); aliases work; iTerm2 executes command

Direction fixes (terminology varies per terminal):
- Zellij:  --direction right  (was 'down')
- Tmux:    -h unchanged       (tmux -h = left|right, correct)
- Kitty:   --type=hsplit      (was 'horizontal'; hsplit = vertical divider = left|right)
- WezTerm: --horizontal unchanged (WezTerm --horizontal = vertical divider)
- iTerm2:  'split vertically' (was 'split horizontally'; 'vertically' = vertical line = left|right)

Alias support:
- All terminals now spawn: shell -i -c 'cmd'  so ~/.zshrc/.bashrc aliases are loaded
- iTerm2 AppleScript: saves newSession ref and calls 'write text' on it directly
  (fixes: command not executing in new pane)
- iTerm2 AppleScript: added 'activate' and correct escaping for cwd/cmd

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: 修复 goroutine 泄漏和 HTTP 慢查询问题

主要修复：

1. Rotator 异步写入优化
   - 使用 channel 缓冲写入请求，避免阻塞 TLS 握手
   - 单独的协程处理文件 I/O 操作
   - 非阻塞发送，通道满时丢弃日志（优先保证性能）

2. HTTP Transport 连接池优化
   - 创建共享的 HTTP Transport 配置 (internal/httputil)
   - 统一管理所有 HTTP 客户端的连接池参数
   - 修复 fetchPublicIPv4 的 KeepAlive: 0 问题
   - 设置合理的 MaxIdleConns、MaxIdleConnsPerHost 等参数

3. pprof 路由修复
   - 将 pprof 路由移到最前面，避免被前端 SPA 拦截
   - 添加所有 pprof 端点的独立路由
   - 本地访问安全检查

相关问题:
- 修复 JS 文件加载 6+ 秒的慢查询
- 修复 monitoring/metrics API 7-10 秒的响应时间
- 解决 goroutine 泄漏（500+ 泄漏）

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add gog-router shadow wrapper for structural command enforcement

Amber ignores wrapper script instructions and calls raw `gog` despite
rules in 4+ config files. This is a known OpenClaw community issue —
prompt-level enforcement is unreliable. The shadow wrapper intercepts
all `gog` calls via pathPrepend and routes reads/tags to the correct
wrapper scripts automatically. Sends still pass through to real binary.

commit.message:
fix(clemens): distinguish logic errors (CRITICAL) from defensive coding (HIGH)

Both code-reviewer and slim-code-reviewer now have clear severity
definitions that separate:

- CRITICAL: Logic errors — algorithm produces wrong output for VALID
  input (e.g. Feb 29 recurring holiday silently becomes March 1)
- HIGH: Defensive coding — code crashes on INVALID/unexpected input
  (e.g. parseTimeToMinutes(null) throws TypeError)

Previously both were HIGH, leading to CONDITIONAL verdict for actual
bugs. Now logic errors trigger REJECTED → mandatory auto-fix.

commit.message:
Fix scheduler to skip already-assigned beads in next_assignable_tasks

Previously, when no in-progress task declared globs the function returned
all ready beads — including ones already being worked on. This moves the
"no locked globs" short-circuit into the per-bead filter and adds an
explicit in-progress ID check so a bead is never double-assigned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix rotate: add geographic diversity when no country filter is set

Without this, score-based selection always picked the globally best-scoring
country (Switzerland in practice), making rotate feel broken even though it
was technically working (different server, same country).

Now: when no country/city filter is set and the provider exposes a server
list, rotate picks a random country (excluding the current one) first, then
score-selects within that country. Filter-constrained rotation is unchanged.

- 7 new tests in tests/test_rotate_diversity.py

commit.message:
Add vector-memory plugin

Local vector-based memory with automatic fact extraction and context injection.

Features:
- Semantic search via Ollama nomic-embed-text (768 dims)
- Automatic fact extraction from tool results and sessions
- Context injection via before_agent_start hook
- Local-first: node:sqlite (Node 22+) + Ollama

Hooks:
- before_agent_start: Inject relevant memories into context
- agent_end: Extract outcomes from completed sessions
- after_tool_call: Extract facts from tool results

commit.message:
add: Theorem G'' (Bloch decomposition) with exact validation

Derives closed-form rank formula for placement sheaf on periodic grids
using Fourier/Bloch decomposition. Key results:
- rank(δ) = Σ_k rank(δ̂(k)), exact on torus
- rank(δ̂(k)) = dim span of active neighbor directions at k
- Three phases: H-only (gap large), H+V (gap moderate), diagonal (gap ≈ 0)
- Diagonal threshold at r = √(a²+b²) eliminates all rank-1 k-points
- Validated: Bloch = SVD on all 18 synthetic grid configurations

Also includes Gemini review fixes to Theorem G:
- Laman reference, transversality (G3), Schur complement proof
- Scalar sheaf decomposition for G'
- PDK-qualified 99% claim
